notes: The 0.720 value is the deterministic strict lower bound; the official-prompt-compatible stored-answer diagnostic is reported separately and is not directly comparable to published official scores.
Current external references use different answer models and retrieval budgets, so they are not controlled head-to-head measurements.

run.command: eval:phase-62-full500 -- --benchmark-root <dir with longmemeval_s.json = HF longmemeval_s_cleaned> --profile goodmemory-rules-only --profile baseline-no-memory --shard-concurrency 4 --run-id run-phase67b-longmemeval-rules-deterministic-current (resumable via --resume-existing-shards; auto-merges) -> eval:phase-62-deterministic-subset -- --report-path <runDir>/report.json --claim-profile goodmemory-rules-only

assertions.equals: The 0.888 stored-answer diagnostic used pinned LongMemEval prompts with gpt-5.4, but the historical artifact did not persist provider and gateway identity; it is not directly comparable to published official scores.

artifacts.description: Tracked compact projection of the historical LongMemEval evidence boundary and source-artifact hashes

metrics.primary: Judge-free deterministic-subset answer accuracy, goodmemory-rules-only profile, full 500 questions: a case counts correct ONLY when scored by a deterministic method (abstention/exact/contains/expected_alternative/numeric_count); semantic_judge is excluded by construction. Strict lower bound on overall accuracy.

coverage.note: Full 500 questions, both profiles, executionFailures 0 (run-phase67b-longmemeval-rules-deterministic-current, 2026-07-02). Claim profile goodmemory-rules-only: 360/500 judge-free correct = exact 163 + contains 118 + numeric_count 50 + abstention 28 + expected_alternative 1 (abstention is only 7.8% of the judge-free correct — the claim is overwhelmingly content matches, not I-don't-know credit). Baseline-no-memory: 34/500 = 0.068, composed of abstention 30 + exact 3 + contains 1 — without memory the model can do little but correctly abstain. Memory lift +65.2 points. Diagnostics NOT claimed as the primary: same-model semantic_judge rescued 88 further mismatches (overall 0.896); evidence-session recall 0.9543. PROMPT-COMPATIBLE TRACK 2026-07-05 (rescore of the SAME stored hypotheses, no regeneration; scripts/rescore-official-protocols.ts, run rescore-longmemeval-official-judge): under the official LongMemEval evaluate_qa.py anscheck prompts (temperature 0), judged by gpt-5.4 (cross-version, same family as the gpt-5.5 answerer - the primary gateway serves no other family), accuracy 0.888 (444/500, judgeFailures 0); per type: single-session-user 1.000, knowledge-update 0.936, multi-session 0.880, single-session-assistant 0.857, temporal-reasoning 0.842, preference 0.800. The pinned evaluator model zoo accepts gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, and llama-3.1-70b-instruct, not gpt-5.4; therefore this diagnostic is not directly comparable to published official scores such as Zep's gpt-4o-judged 90.2. The judge-free 0.720 remains the strict lower bound.

claimBoundary.reason: Historical evidence only for its recorded package version and runtime profile; it is not a current-production claim. The recorded judge-free metric excludes semantic_judge rescues, reports executionFailures 0 on all 500x2 cases, and binds the tracked projection to the full source commit and source-artifact fingerprints. Current-package status, profile availability, and historical classification intentionally block promotion. Required disclosures if reconsidered: the profile is embedding-free goodmemory-rules-only; the optional same-model gpt-5.5 semantic_judge contributes zero to the claimed 0.72; answers are generated by gpt-5.5, so judge-free refers only to scoring; and the historical 0.908 with-judge result is superseded.
